## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### US Government Shuts Down Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Over Jailbreak Risk

The US government ordered Anthropic to disable worldwide access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 12, 2026, marking the first government intervention triggered by an AI system deemed "too powerful." The shutdown follows discovery of a jailbreak technique that allows users to bypass safety guardrails by requesting the models read codebases and fix software flaws. Anthropic disputes the severity, characterizing the vulnerability as a narrow, non-universal exploit comparable to risks in other frontier models.

**Why it matters.** This precedent reshapes how frontier AI companies navigate deployment. The government's invocation of national security authority for AI safety (rather than export control or traditional security) signals regulatory bodies will intervene mid-deployment if frontier capability outpaces safeguards. For teams building agentic systems on external APIs, this undercuts the assumption that third-party frontier models offer stable long-term access—governments can revoke service at scale. Anthropic's inability to retain access to its own production models despite its safety mitigations challenges the "we can self-regulate" narrative that underpins current industry trust assumptions.

**Action.** If you're operating production systems on Fable 5 or Mythos 5, inventory dependencies now and prepare fallback paths to Claude Opus 4.8, Gemini 2.0, or open-weight alternatives. For agentic systems, test your orchestration layer's ability to swap frontier providers without retraining. If safety research is your focus, Anthropic's public contestation of the jailbreak's severity is worth a close read—the technical disagreement between a safety-forward company and US intelligence on what constitutes "jailbreak-level risk" will set precedent for future interventions.

*Sources: [[Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-13/anthropic-says-us-limits-foreign-access-to-fable-5-mythos-5)]([Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-13/anthropic-says-us-limits-foreign-access-to-fable-5-mythos-5)) · [[TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/ai-platforms-assistants/claude/after-a-potential-jailbreak-anthropic-is-shutting-off-access-to-its-mythos-5-and-fable-5-models-under-national-security-orders-from-the-us-government)]([TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/ai-platforms-assistants/claude/after-a-potential-jailbreak-anthropic-is-shutting-off-access-to-its-mythos-5-and-fable-5-models-under-national-security-orders-from-the-us-government)) · [[Geeky Gadgets](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/anthropic-fable-5-government-shutdown/)]([Geeky Gadgets](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/anthropic-fable-5-government-shutdown/))*

### Moonshot Releases Kimi K2.7 Code—Open-Weight Model Cuts Reasoning Tokens 30% While Raising Code Benchmarks

Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code on June 12, 2026, a 1 trillion-parameter open-weight model optimized for long-horizon agentic software engineering. The model achieves a 21.8% improvement on Kimi Code Bench v2 (scoring 62.0 vs 50.9 on K2.6) while reducing reasoning token usage by approximately 30%. With a 256K-token context window and MIT licensing, K2.7 Code ships on Hugging Face at $0.95/$4.00 per million input/output tokens on API.

**Why it matters.** This release demonstrates the frontier for open-weight code models has shifted decisively toward agentic reasoning and efficiency. A 21.8% benchmark jump with lower token burn means open-weight models are now competitive on the dimension that matters most for tool-using agents: solving multi-file refactors and long-context reasoning on unfamiliar codebases without burning through context budget. For teams self-hosting, this eliminates much of the business-model risk you'd face with proprietary APIs—you get agentic capability without government shutdown risk or API vendor lock-in.

**Action.** Benchmark K2.7 Code against your internal software engineering workflows. The 256K context window and ~30% lower reasoning-token cost versus K2.6 make it worth a direct A/B swap on your longest-context tasks (multi-file refactors, large codebase migration planning). If you're running Claude or Gemini code models in production, run K2.7 Code on a representative workload—the open-weight model may close the capability gap while letting you own the serving layer and avoid regulatory surprises.

*Sources: [[MarkTechPost](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/06/12/moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k2-7-code-a-coding-model-reporting-21-8-on-kimi-code-bench-v2-over-k2-6-)]([MarkTechPost](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/06/12/moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k2-7-code-a-coding-model-reporting-21-8-on-kimi-code-bench-v2-over-k2-6-)) · [[Crypto Briefing](https://cryptobriefing.com/kimi-k2-7-code-open-source-release/)]([Crypto Briefing](https://cryptobriefing.com/kimi-k2-7-code-open-source-release/)) · [[Kingy AI](https://kingy.ai/ai/kimi-k2-7-code-benchmarks-specs/)]([Kingy AI](https://kingy.ai/ai/kimi-k2-7-code-benchmarks-specs/))*

## 🇮🇳 India

### PM Modi, Macron Launch Bharat Innovates 2026 Showcasing 120 Indian Deeptech Startups

Prime Minister Narendra Modi and French President Emmanuel Macron jointly inaugurated Bharat Innovates 2026 on June 14 in Nice, France—a three-day platform bringing together 120 Indian deeptech startups, 15 higher education institutions, and over 500 investors and global CEOs. The event targets 13 frontier technology areas including semiconductors, biotechnology, space and defence, healthcare, advanced computing, and next-generation communications. Participants include Indian chip designers like Agnit Semiconductors (GaN RF), BigEndian Semiconductors (AI vision chips), and quantum-security players QpiAI and QNu Labs.

**Why it matters.** For AI engineers in India building sovereign tech stacks—whether in semiconductors, quantum, or AI—this is the validation moment. Bharat Innovates positions deeptech as central to India-France bilateral cooperation and signals sustained government backing for capital-intensive moonshot companies that international VCs historically shun. Direct access to 500+ investors from one platform cuts deal-flow friction for founders working on AI chips, biotech, and defence tech. The frontier tech list (no e-commerce, fintech, or quick commerce) clarifies where public capital wants to concentrate through the IndiaAI Mission and state backing.

**Action.** Monitor announcements from June 14-16 for breakout funding rounds and commitments announced at Nice. Track which of the 120 participating startups secure international partnerships, particularly with French and EU tech firms. Pull the official Bharat Innovates participant list (published by DPIIT) to identify companies moving from bootstrapped to Series A territory. Flag semiconductor and biotech rounds—these fund $50M+ and move slowly; catching them early helps teams planning hiring and supplier contracts.

*Sources: [[Bharat Innovates 2026: Modi and Macron Launch India-France Deep Tech Platform](https://currentaffairs.adda247.com/bharat-innovates-2026-pm-modi-and-macron-to-launch-india-france-deep-tech-platform/amp/)]([Bharat Innovates 2026: Modi and Macron Launch India-France Deep Tech Platform](https://currentaffairs.adda247.com/bharat-innovates-2026-pm-modi-and-macron-to-launch-india-france-deep-tech-platform/amp/)) · [[PM Modi and Macron to Jointly Inaugurate Bharat Innovates 2026 on June 14](https://www.thestatesman.com/india/modi-macron-to-jointly-inaugurate-bharat-innovates-2026-on-june-14-in-nice-france-1503605087.html)]([PM Modi and Macron to Jointly Inaugurate Bharat Innovates 2026 on June 14](https://www.thestatesman.com/india/modi-macron-to-jointly-inaugurate-bharat-innovates-2026-on-june-14-in-nice-france-1503605087.html)) · [[Modi's France Visit Puts India's Deeptech Ambitions in Focus](https://www.businesstoday.in/technology/story/from-nice-to-paris-pm-modis-france-visit-puts-indias-deeptech-ambitions-in-focus-at-bharat-innovates-and-vivatech-536558-2026-06-12)]([Modi's France Visit Puts India's Deeptech Ambitions in Focus](https://www.businesstoday.in/technology/story/from-nice-to-paris-pm-modis-france-visit-puts-indias-deeptech-ambitions-in-focus-at-bharat-innovates-and-vivatech-536558-2026-06-12))*

### Zepto Files ₹8,010 Crore IPO, Revealing Widened Losses Amid Scaling

Quick commerce unicorn Zepto filed its updated DRHP with SEBI on June 9, planning to raise ₹8,010 crore in fresh equity while early investors including Nexus Ventures and Contrary Capital exit partial stakes. FY26 financials disclosed in the filing show revenue doubled to ₹22,623 crore but net losses widened to ₹5,905 crore—a stark reminder that growth-at-all-costs hasn't yet flipped to profitability. The IPO opens roadshows imminently, targeting a listing in late June or July 2026.

**Why it matters.** Zepto's IPO trajectory tests whether Indian public markets will absorb super-scale, pre-profitable logistics players—the answer directly shapes term sheets for any founder building on last-mile infrastructure, dark stores, or supply-chain AI. A successful listing at valuation justified by growth traction alone legitimizes runway extension as a path forward; a failed IPO or deep repricing triggers VCs to demand unit economics proof earlier. For engineers considering offers from Zepto or similar logistics-tech players, this filing clarifies the runway: every crore raised is capital for next 18–24 months at current burn. The widened loss narrative also signals intensifying competition (Amazon Freshaggressive, D2C startups fractionalizing delivery).

**Action.** Read the full DRHP on SEBI's website (updated draft filed June 9, 2026) and cross-reference dark store density, CAC/LTV by geography, and monthly active user trends against Q1 FY25 disclosures—look for slowdown signals. Track roadshow commentary from institutional investors on Twitter and fintech forums for pushback on path-to-profitability. Compare Zepto's loss-to-revenue ratio (26%) against Indian-listed quick commerce and logistics peers (Shadowfax, Ecomm Express relative valuations). Flag for portfolio companies: if Zepto's IPO reprices downward, expect Series B/C terms to compress within 6 weeks.

*Sources: [[Zepto Files Updated DRHP for ₹8,010 Crore Fresh Issue](https://www.outlookbusiness.com/corporate/zepto-files-updated-drhp-for-8010-cr-fresh-issue-investors-eye-partial-exit)]([Zepto Files Updated DRHP for ₹8,010 Crore Fresh Issue](https://www.outlookbusiness.com/corporate/zepto-files-updated-drhp-for-8010-cr-fresh-issue-investors-eye-partial-exit)) · [[Zepto Files U-DRHP with SEBI; Plans to Raise ₹8,010 Crore](https://groww.in/blog/upcoming-ipo-zepto-files-u-drhp-with-sebi-plans-to-raise-8010-crore-via-fresh-issue)]([Zepto Files U-DRHP with SEBI; Plans to Raise ₹8,010 Crore](https://groww.in/blog/upcoming-ipo-zepto-files-u-drhp-with-sebi-plans-to-raise-8010-crore-via-fresh-issue)) · [[Zepto IPO DRHP: User Base Dips, Marketing Costs Surge FY26](https://univest.in/blogs/zepto-ipo-user-base-marketing-costs)]([Zepto IPO DRHP: User Base Dips, Marketing Costs Surge FY26](https://univest.in/blogs/zepto-ipo-user-base-marketing-costs))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### ARM (NASDAQ): +11.3% on June 12 after record Q4 earnings and Arm AGI CPU ecosystem wins

ARM Holdings jumped $38.58 to $380.81 on June 12, 2026, a gain of 11.27% that extended into June 13 trading. The British chip design firm reported record fiscal year 2026 revenue of $4.92 billion (up 23% YoY) and Q4 net income of $1.77 per share, both beating expectations. The catalyst: the Arm AGI CPU launched at Computex in early June drew immediate design wins from Oracle and ByteDance, while customer demand for the new architecture doubled from the prior $1 billion estimate to over $2 billion across fiscal 2027–2028. Volume trading surged 45% above 30-day average.

**Why it matters.** ARM's leap signals the CPU design space is tilting toward AI inference workloads, not just training accelerators. For the semiconductor supply chain, this means foundries like TSMC and Samsung now face demand from two flanks—GPUs from NVDA/AMD and CPUs from ARM—both competing for advanced node capacity. For Indian semiconductor and fabless startups, the move validates that custom silicon for AI is no longer limited to NVDA and AVGO; design wins are broadening into processor ecosystems where ARM controls the instruction set. The shift also implies longer-term diversification away from GPU-only bets, a meaningful read-across for system-on-chip vendors and EDA tool providers.

**Action.** Review ARM's Q4 FY2026 earnings transcript (https://ir.arm.com/) for detailed customer wins and FY2027 guidance. Watch the $400.00 resistance level; a break above confirms the escape from the earlier June correction. Also monitor whether AVGO and NVDA acknowledge ARM competition in their next investor calls, or dismiss it as incremental. Key filing: ARM Form 20-F with full-year results.

*Sources: [[ARM Holdings PLC Stock (ARM) Moved Up by 6.16% on Jun 12](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/261964452-market-movers-arm-20260612)]([ARM Holdings PLC Stock (ARM) Moved Up by 6.16% on Jun 12](https://www.tradingkey.com/news/market-movers/261964452-market-movers-arm-20260612)) · [[ARM Stock Rockets On AI Price Target Hikes And New Server Deals](https://stockstotrade.com/news/arm-holdings-plc-arm-news-2026_06_12/)]([ARM Stock Rockets On AI Price Target Hikes And New Server Deals](https://stockstotrade.com/news/arm-holdings-plc-arm-news-2026_06_12/)) · [[ARM Holdings Rose 11% Today. Here's Where the Stock Is Headed in 2026](https://www.tikr.com/blog/arm-holdings-rose-11-today-heres-where-the-stock-is-headed-in-2026)]([ARM Holdings Rose 11% Today. Here's Where the Stock Is Headed in 2026](https://www.tikr.com/blog/arm-holdings-rose-11-today-heres-where-the-stock-is-headed-in-2026))*

### ASML (EURONEXT: ASML): €8.8B Q1 revenue beat, guidance raised to €36–40B on fab acceleration wave

ASML Holding reported €8.8 billion in first-quarter 2026 net sales on April 16, beating consensus by 3.5%, with gross margin expanding to 53.0% and earnings per share of €7.15 versus the €6.00 consensus estimate. More significantly, the Dutch lithography equipment giant raised its full-year 2026 net sales guidance to €36–40 billion (from €34–39 billion), citing TSMC, Intel, and Samsung all accelerating capital equipment orders in response to surging AI chip demand. EUV system sales reached €4.1 billion within total system sales of €6.3 billion, signaling that customers are front-loading orders ahead of supply constraints. The stock gained 7.2% in the week following the announcement, closing near session highs as of June 13.

**Why it matters.** ASML's guidance raise is the clearest signal yet that foundries view the AI chip shortage as structural, not cyclical. For 2026–2027, TSMC, Samsung, and Intel are committing capex equivalent to building out global AI compute capacity that would support multi-year demand for data-center GPUs, custom training silicon, and inference accelerators. This tightens the semiconductor supply chain bottleneck around advanced process nodes (3nm and below), which directly impacts NVDA, AMD, AVGO, and every fabless AI chip start-up. For Indian semiconductor players, ASML's guidance raise validates that there is no near-term relief in advanced node availability—securing foundry slots remains the critical blocker.

**Action.** Read ASML's Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (https://www.asml.com/en/investors/financial-results) to identify which customer geographies are seeing the largest capex acceleration. Watch for management commentary on the timing of EUV capacity constraints; any mention of multi-quarter lead times on tools confirms the supply/demand imbalance. Cross-reference with TSMC and Samsung guidance due in coming weeks. Key metric to track: the ratio of EUV to DUV orders, which signals customer willingness to pay for the most advanced node density.

*Sources: [[ASML reports €8.8 billion total net sales and €2.8 billion net income in Q1 2026](https://www.asml.com/en/news/press-releases/2026/q1-2026-financial-results)]([ASML reports €8.8 billion total net sales and €2.8 billion net income in Q1 2026](https://www.asml.com/en/news/press-releases/2026/q1-2026-financial-results)) · [[ASML Q1 2026 Earnings: €8.8B Revenue and €40B Guidance Raise](https://tech-insider.org/asml-q1-2026-revenue-earnings-guidance-raise-ai-chip/)]([ASML Q1 2026 Earnings: €8.8B Revenue and €40B Guidance Raise](https://tech-insider.org/asml-q1-2026-revenue-earnings-guidance-raise-ai-chip/)) · [[TSMC and ASML post-earnings stock moves could be a sign of what's to come from chip companies](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/taiwan-semi-tsm-asml-stock-earnings-ai-chips.html)]([TSMC and ASML post-earnings stock moves could be a sign of what's to come from chip companies](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/taiwan-semi-tsm-asml-stock-earnings-ai-chips.html))*

## 🏗 Tech & Startups

### SpaceX Debuts as the Largest IPO in History at $1.77 Trillion Valuation

SpaceX went public on June 13, 2026, priced at $135 per share on June 12, raising approximately $75 billion in what became the largest initial public offering in history. The company debuted on Nasdaq under ticker SPCX, valuing the space-based AI infrastructure play at $1.77 trillion. The IPO marks a watershed moment for private space ventures and reflects investor appetite for infrastructure backing AI compute demands.

**Why it matters.** SpaceX's debut signals institutional confidence that satellite-based data center infrastructure is a solved problem worth trillions. For founders building in space tech, deeptech, and AI infrastructure, the IPO validates that hard-problem solvers with defensible physics and government contracts can command mythical valuations. The deal shows capital is willing to fund decade-long bets on moving compute off-planet.

**Action.** Builders in satellite communications, orbital manufacturing, and space-to-ground data links should map their competitive position against SpaceX's advantages and thesis. Study the S-1 filing for Musk's stated path to profitability via data center contracts—it's the playbook other space-infrastructure founders will copy.

*Sources: [[IPOX Update 6/13/2026](https://www.ipox.com/ipox/the-ipox-update-06132026)]([IPOX Update 6/13/2026](https://www.ipox.com/ipox/the-ipox-update-06132026)) · [[Top Tech News June 12, 2026](https://techstartups.com/2026/06/12/top-tech-news-today-june-12-2026/)]([Top Tech News June 12, 2026](https://techstartups.com/2026/06/12/top-tech-news-today-june-12-2026/)) · [[Upcoming IPOs 2026](https://dealroom.net/blog/upcoming-recent-ipos)]([Upcoming IPOs 2026](https://dealroom.net/blog/upcoming-recent-ipos))*

### Ethereal Machines Raises $28.5M Series B to Build India's First Indigenous CNC Controller

Ethereal Machines closed a $28.5 million Series B led by Avataar Ventures on June 11, 2026, bringing the Bengaluru-based precision manufacturing startup to $86 million in total funding. Founded in 2014 by Kaushik Mudda and Navin Jain, the company has grown its Machining-as-a-Service (MaaS) revenue threefold year-over-year and scaled production capacity tenfold since its $13 million Series A in June 2024.

**Why it matters.** Ethereal Machines represents the deeptech manufacturing playbook that works: solve a hard technical problem (multi-axis CNC), build a services moat to fund R&D, then build proprietary hardware. The company's indigenous controller project signals India's bet on semiconductor and precision manufacturing independence. For founders in hardware and advanced manufacturing, this shows that combining software (AI factory software) with capital-intensive hardware attracts growth-stage capital at scale.

**Action.** Study how Ethereal structured MaaS as a revenue engine while building the CNC controller in parallel—it's the model reducing hardware founder dilution. Track their expansion into the US and Europe; their playbook for exporting deeptech from India to Western manufacturers is worth following.

*Sources: [[Ethereal Machines Series B - Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/news/ethereal-machines-raises-285-mn-in-series-b-round-led-by-avataar-ventures-12023907)]([Ethereal Machines Series B - Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/news/ethereal-machines-raises-285-mn-in-series-b-round-led-by-avataar-ventures-12023907)) · [[Ethereal Machines on Indian Startup News](https://indianstartupnews.com/funding/ethereal-machines-raises-285-million-to-build-bengaluru-manufacturing-facility-expand-ai-factory-software-12027332)]([Ethereal Machines on Indian Startup News](https://indianstartupnews.com/funding/ethereal-machines-raises-285-million-to-build-bengaluru-manufacturing-facility-expand-ai-factory-software-12027332)) · [[Business Standard Coverage](https://www.business-standard.com/amp/companies/start-ups/ethereal-machines-raises-28-5-million-to-expand-precision-manufacturing-126061100542_1.html)]([Business Standard Coverage](https://www.business-standard.com/amp/companies/start-ups/ethereal-machines-raises-28-5-million-to-expand-precision-manufacturing-126061100542_1.html))*
